audit_scope: Independent release correctness, provenance, supply-chain, licensing, data-boundary, packaging, documentation, and claims review

findings.title: Project and CLI identified as 2.0.0 for a requested 1.0.0 release
findings.closure: CMake is the 1.0.0 source of truth; CLI, citation, changelog, release notes, packages, and automated consistency tests agree.

findings.title: Compatible-descendant reconstruction accepted arbitrary post-manifest code changes
findings.closure: Executable provenance closure limits implementation-to-manifest changes to manifests/ and later changes to audit/release_v1/ evidence only.

findings.title: No transactional cross-platform release packaging and validation path
findings.closure: Exact-SHA Linux/macOS packaging, target-runner smoke tests, archive scans, source bundle, SBOM, checksums, and aggregate validation are implemented without automatic publication.

findings.title: Python distributions were version-pinned but not hash-locked
findings.closure: Validation and optional acquisition environments have separate pip-compile locks with distribution hashes, verified installs, inventory equality tests, and CI --require-hashes.

findings.title: Exact-SHA release workflows used a shallow checkout that omitted provenance ancestors
findings.closure: All workflow steps that execute release provenance closure now fetch complete ancestry. The validator was not weakened, and manifests were recaptured from the corrected workflow boundary.

findings.title: Strict-warning mode permitted warnings
findings.closure: Proven safe size conversions are explicit and supported GCC/Clang/MSVC warning modes use warnings-as-errors. Financial formulas are unchanged.

findings.title: No current release citation, security, contribution, or immutability policy
findings.closure: CITATION.cff, SECURITY.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, and docs/RELEASE_POLICY.md are present and link/version tested.

findings.title: Operating-system toolchains are recorded but not hermetically vendored
findings.closure: Linux/macOS runner tool versions are recorded in release assets; semantic cross-platform validation is required and the limitation is documented.

findings.title: Historical commits retain removed third-party market data
findings.closure: No history rewrite is performed. Current-tree and release-archive validators reject paths, full hashes, and sampled rows; the historical boundary is documented.
